source_note: Deepened from user-supplied closing-line screenshots, official scorer report, player tracking rebounding, player box outs, and uploaded Fouls/Rebounds/Scoring/Shot Distribution/Turnovers text files.

closing_market_context.total_range: 215.5 to 216.5, with best odds around O215.5 -110 / U216 -102 on screen

team_totals.CLE: Mostly 109.5, with Circa 108.5, DraftKings 110.5, Bovada/Rebet 109.0; Kalshi 109.5 -111 over / -108 under

scoring.interpretation: Cleveland had slightly better modeled shot quality but New York converted perimeter and free-throw value more efficiently. CLE's 29.27% from three on 41 attempts created the team-total failure point.

shot_distribution.interpretation: CLE reached the right zones but was overly dependent on high-volume above-break threes. NYK's lower rim-or-3 frequency was offset by cleaner finishing, corner shooting, and FT conversion.

rebounds.interpretation: Rebounding was close by team totals, but KAT's ladder path failed because NYK boards dispersed through Hart, Bridges, OG, Robinson minutes, and team/wing rebounds.

turnovers.interpretation: CLE's 18 turnovers and 10 bad passes are the clearest creation-fragility signal. Live-ball turnover rate amplified NYK transition/control advantages.

fouls.interpretation: CLE committed more fouls while drawing fewer. NYK converted the whistle into 24 FT points; CLE only produced 12 FT points.

player_tracking_rebounding.rebound_lesson: KAT had 19 chances but converted only 8 rebounds, making 12+ a bad ladder without live confirmation of isolated rebound funnel. Hart/OG/Bridges absorbed enough NYK boards to flatten the distribution.

player_box_outs.CLE_big_context: Mobley and Allen each had only one box out. CLE did not create a strong two-big board seal advantage even with Allen/Mobley minutes.

liveflow_entries.diagnosis: LATE_TOTAL_OVERFLOW / Knicks efficiency and 4Q shotmaking exceeded suppression model

portfolio_result.net_known: positive due to larger CLE TT position

phase_1_game_phase_analysis.q1: NYK won the opening phase 37-27 behind 70.6% FG, 50% from three, and 9/10 at the line. CLE's 27 points kept them alive, but the offense was already narrow: paint makes, Mitchell/Harden usage, poor perimeter rhythm, and limited transition.
phase_1_game_phase_analysis.q2: CLE won Q2 27-23 and tied at 48, but NYK closed the half 12-6. The critical note is that Cleveland recovered margin but not structure: 9 first-half turnovers, 4/19 from three, and just 8 assists kept their team-total over fragile.
phase_1_game_phase_analysis.q3: NYK won Q3 31-28. Cleveland again reached scoring volume but could not flip control because early third-quarter bad passes by Mobley/Harden/Mitchell created empty possessions and NYK runouts. Brunson and Bridges repeatedly answered each CLE push.
phase_1_game_phase_analysis.q4: NYK won Q4 30-26. Shamet's fourth-quarter spacing burst broke the full-game under while Cleveland landed exactly in the team-total under pocket. Final 121-108: NYK side, NYK TT over, full-game over, CLE TT under all validated together.

phase_2_pivotal_moments: NYK 37-point Q1 created immediate chase state and forced CLE into higher-variance perimeter volume.
CLE tied 48-48 in Q2, but Knicks closed the half up 60-54 and never surrendered control.
CLE third-quarter live-ball turnovers stopped comeback pressure even when their scoring remained functional.
Brunson/Bridges stabilized every Cleveland mini-run with efficient midrange/rim scoring.
Shamet's fourth-quarter threes drove the final total over 222.5 despite CLE team-total under surviving.
CLE finished at 108, making the 109.5 under a one-possession but structurally correct edge.

phase_3_defensive_psychological_variable.cle_creation_fragility: The uploaded turnover profile shows CLE with 18 turnovers, 11 live-ball turnovers, and 10 bad passes. That is not random noise; it is ball-security and decision fatigue under Knicks pressure.
phase_3_defensive_psychological_variable.nyk_composure: NYK had 15 turnovers but still produced 121 because their scoring lanes were more diversified: Brunson creation, Bridges finishing, Towns connective passing, Hart/OG physicality, and Shamet spacing.
phase_3_defensive_psychological_variable.rebound_distribution: The KAT rebound ladder failed because Towns' 19 chances did not translate into a dominant individual share. Hart had 9 rebounds on 12 chances; OG had 7; Bridges had 6; Robinson had 3 in limited minutes.
phase_3_defensive_psychological_variable.free_throw_delta: NYK generated 24 FT points vs CLE's 12. Cleveland's missed free throws were the hidden point tax that kept their team total below 109.5.
phase_3_defensive_psychological_variable.closing_pressure: Cleveland never had a home lead. Chasing all night increased reliance on Harden/Mitchell creation and above-break threes, which produced volume but not enough efficiency.

what_model_got_right: CLE TT under was the best live read and won at 108.
Cleveland creation fragility was real: 18 TO, 10 bad passes, weak transition, poor FT conversion.
NYK offensive profile was more durable than the market implied; Knicks scored 121 and won outright as underdog.
CLE team-total under could coexist with full-game over because NYK offense was the total driver.
what_model_got_wrong: KAT 12+ rebound ladder overweighted chances without enough individual conversion context.
Full-game under 222.5 underpriced NYK late shotmaking and Shamet bench volatility.
Lead-state suppression was over-applied to the total; playoff chase state plus NYK efficiency kept scoring live.
CLE's poor three-point efficiency was correctly read for team total, but NYK's perimeter efficiency was not suppressed enough.
phase_4_model_relevance_summary.game_4_prep_read: For Game 4, keep CLE_CREATION_FRAGILITY active but do not blindly attack full-game under. NYK side, NYK TT, or CLE TT under may be cleaner than total under. KAT rebound ladders require live chance-conversion confirmation. Cleveland can clear good shot-quality metrics and still underperform if above-break threes and FT conversion remain weak.

rules: Separate CLE team-total under from full-game under when NYK offensive efficiency is above baseline.
Do not ladder KAT rebounds above 10.5/11.5 unless live rebound chances are both concentrated and converting above 55%.
If CLE 3PA rate remains high with AB3 dependence, downgrade CLE TT overs even if rim finishing is strong.
If NYK gets 20+ FT attempts and 35%+ from three, NYK TT over is live even against slow pace.
Bench shooter volatility must be explicitly priced when Shamet/Merrill/Strus types are in closing or extended rotations.

model_grade.read_quality: high on CLE team total, high on NYK side/TT after closing context, medium-low on full-game total, low-medium on KAT rebounds
model_grade.market_selection: mixed but profitable on primary stake weighting
model_grade.execution_quality: positive due to primary edge win and correct CLE fragility read

next_actions: Use this file as Game 4 prep input before tip off.
Run Game 4 simulation with CLE creation-fragility and NYK multi-lane offense tags active.
Scan Game 4 markets for NYK side, NYK TT over, CLE TT under, and avoid automatic total-under correlation.
Require live rebound tracking before any KAT ladder exposure.
